channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Zelenskyy says he will only agree to meet with Putin after common plan with Trump is negotiated
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will only agree to meet in-person with Russian leader Vladimir Putin after a common plan is negotiated with U.S. President Trump. Zelenskyy believes Trump is the key to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, and said the U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern California hit by destructive debris flows caused by heavy rains
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Debris flows brought on by the strongest atmospheric river of the season swept a car off a southern California highway and into the ocean and left roads submerged in sludge.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KREX 5

item.title: Very cold weather is coming. US about to get 10th and chilliest polar vortex this winter
item.description: The coldest burst of Arctic air this season is coming to put an icy exclamation point on America's winter of repeated polar vortex invasions, meteorologists warn. And it will stay frozen there all next week.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Annexing Canada? At Niagara Falls, Trump's idea gets eye rolls but not support
item.description: NIAGARA FALLS, N.Y. (AP) — At Niagara Falls, the iconic honeymoon and tourist destination on the U.S.-Canadian border, visitors from both countries took in President Donald Trump’s unsolicited suggestion that the U.S. annex its neighbor to the north with an air of detached amusement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Would-be Indiana school shooter had collage of mass murderers, court docs reveal
item.description: The student said in January that the shooting would be “Parkland part two” and that they had been planning it for a year.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US aircraft carrier collides with merchant vessel in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: The collision occurred between the Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) and the merchant vessel Besiktas-M, according to U.S. Navy spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fourth federal judge blocks Trump’s birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Thursday blocked an executive order from President Donald Trump that would end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, becoming the fourth judge to do so. The ruling from U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man injured alongside Salman Rushdie in 2022 stabbing attack says he thought it was a prank at first
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A man injured alongside author Salman Rushdie in a knife attack on a New York lecture stage said Thursday that he tried to stop the assault once he realized it wasn't a prank and was left with a gash above his eye.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAFSA applications down in California amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The share of students with at least one undocumented parent who applied for aid dropped by nearly half.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: More than a dozen state attorneys general challenge Musk and DOGE’s authority
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Attorneys general from 14 states challenged the authority of billionaire Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency to access sensitive government data and exercise “virtually unchecked power" in a lawsuit filed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge pauses President Trump's order restricting gender-affirming care for trans youth
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s recent executive order aimed at restricting gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: AOC's office advises migrants on how to avoid deportations in live webinar
item.description: The office of Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., hosted a live webinar advising migrants in her district about their rights if they come face to face with ICE agents looking to deport them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man whose wife was killed in a hippo attack in Africa sues the US company that booked the trip
item.description: A New Jersey man whose wife was killed in a horrific hippopotamus attack last year during a safari in Africa is suing the U.S. company that arranged the trip, alleging it failed to ensure their safety and did not adequately screen and supervise the tour guides.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man dies after Las Vegas fire crews find him stuck in palm tree
item.description: A caller reported to Las Vegas Fire and Rescue that they saw an unconscious man about 25 feet in the air and stuck in a palm tree.
item.dc:creator: Julia Romero, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor to allow immigration officials to operate at Rikers jail after Trump border czar meeting
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he will allow federal immigration officials to operate at the city’s Rikers Island jail.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Igloo recalls over a million coolers after handle hazard causes fingertip amputation injuries
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Igloo is recalling more than 1 million of its coolers sold across the U.S., Mexico and Canada due to a handle hazard that has resulted in a handful of fingertip injuries, including some amputations. The now-recalled “Igloo 90 Qt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Colorado school district first in country to sue Trump administration over fears of ICE raids on campuses
item.description: The Denver Public Schools system has sued the Trump administration for rescinding its policy restricting ICE raids from taking place on school grounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7-year-old girl flown to Utah hospital after Wyoming mother shoots 4 daughters, herself
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was flown to a Utah hospital with a gunshot wound to the head after a Wyoming mother reportedly shot her four daughters before turning the gun on herself, according to the Big Horn County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two crew members ejected from a Navy jet before it crashed Wednesday off the San Diego coast and were quickly rescued by a sportfishing boat, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Monty the giant schnauzer wins Westminster dog show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — This time, the giant schnauzer went the full Monty. After coming close in the last two years, Monty the giant schnauzer won the top prize at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show Tuesday night, leaving handler and co-owner Katie Bernardin almost too emotional to speak.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inflation ticks up to 3 percent annual increase in January
item.description: The consumer price index (CPI) increased by 0.5 percent in January to hit an annual increase of 3.0 percent after advancing by 2.9 percent in December.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4
